Chicken market projected to become $268.35 billion industry by 2033, up from $160 billion in 2024. Factors include rise of fast food, health consciousness, and plant-based alternatives.
news.summary: A recent report titled "Chicken Market Size and Share Analysis - Growth Trends and Forecast Report 2025-2033" is now available on ResearchAndMarkets.com. The report highlights the projected growth of the Chicken Market, estimating a value of US$160.62 billion in 2024 to US$268.35 billion in 2033, reflecting a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 5.87% during the period 2025 to 2033. This growth is attributed to factors such as increasing global demand for affordable protein, a rise in health consciousness promoting lean meat consumption, the expansion of fast-food and quick-service restaurants, urbanization, and advancements in the sector.

news.headline: Matt Andersen promoted to VP of Sales for Pharmacy Automation at Swisslog Healthcare in North America. Joined 3 years ago.
news.summary: In Broomfield, Colorado, Swisslog Healthcare, a prominent provider of pharmacy and transport automation solutions, has promoted Matt Andersen to Vice President of Sales for Pharmacy Automation in North America. Andersen, a three-year veteran of the company, will take charge of guiding the strategic vision of the North American Pharmacy Automation Sales teams and Clinical Consultants. In his new position, Andersen will oversee the strategic direction of the North American Pharmacy Automation segment.

news.headline: BoC's Gov. Macklem: US tariffs could put downward pressure on Canadian energy prices and reduce the profitability of producers.
BoC's Gov. Macklem: Canadian economy managed a soft landing. Unfortunately, we're not going to stay on the tarmac for long.
Trump and Senate Republicans aim to challenge the Fed's independence using crypto policies as a catalyst.
news.summary: President Trump and Senate Republicans are expected to make strategic decisions regarding cryptocurrency policies with the aim of undermining the Federal Reserve's independence. This move is part of a larger plan to exert control over the Fed’s operations.

news.headline: BoC's Gov. Macklem: Monetary policy must prevent initial direct price increases from spreading. We need to make sure a tariff problem doesn't become an inflation problem.
BoC's Gov. Macklem: There remain too many unknowns about tariffs to predict what happens next.
BoC's Gov. Macklem: The more inflationary the impact of tariffs, the more monetary policy needs to focus on anchoring inflation expectations.

Non-alcoholic spirits market to grow to $1.2 billion in sales by 2034, with a 7.4% CAGR driven by health-conscious consumers. Leading vendors innovating with premium ingredients and complex flavors to meet rising demand. Key players include Kin Euphorics, TÖST, Lyre's Spirit, and Escape Wise Mocktails. United Breweries, AB-InBev, and Carlsberg collaborate to form Brewers' Association of India in May 2024. The Free Spirits Company launches ready-to-drink non-alcoholic California Craft Cocktails in April 2024.
news.summary: The Non-alcoholic Spirits Market is experiencing significant growth, valued at US$531.9 million in 2023 and projected to expand at a CAGR of 7.4%, reaching US$1.2 billion by 2034. This growth is driven by the rising demand for alcohol-free alternatives, as health-conscious consumers seek premium options. Non-alcoholic spirits are crafted using botanicals and distillation techniques to mimic traditional spirit flavors without alcohol. Leading players in the market include Kin Euphorics, ChromaCast, and TÖST, focusing on premiumization, innovative flavors, and expanding distribution channels. Key developments in the industry include collaborations such as the formation of the Brewers' Association of India by United Breweries, AB-InBev, and Carlsberg, and the introduction of new non-alcoholic California Craft Cocktails by The Free Spirits Company in April 2024.

news.headline: BoC's Gov. Macklem: BoC needs to set policy that minimizes the risk of errors; that means being less-forward looking than normal.
BoC's Gov. Macklem: Given high degree of uncertainty about base case, our focus is less on best monetary policy for a specific economic outlook.
BoC's Gov. Macklem: That may also mean acting quickly when things crystallize, but we need to be flexible and adaptable.
BoC's Gov. Macklem reiterates that there can be no doubt about the bank's commitment to low inflation.

Venture capital firms invest $400M in TON blockchain, driving interest in Telegram ecosystem growth. Toncoin sees surge. Blockchains attracting huge capital investments, particularly in FinTech. VP predicts Telegram user base to reach 1.5B by 2030.
news.summary: The Open Network Foundation, also known as TON Foundation, revealed that several venture capital firms invested over $400 million in the TON blockchain, showing increased interest in the Telegram messaging ecosystem. Among the investors were Sequoia Capital, Ribbit, Benchmark, and others, who purchased Toncoin (TON), the network's native cryptocurrency. The TON Foundation aims to onboard 30% of active Telegram users to the blockchain within the next three years, with a goal of expanding the TON ecosystem. The TON blockchain has seen substantial growth in recent times, with native accounts increasing from 4 million to 41 million, and over 121 million unique holders of Toncoin. Telegram's monthly active users doubled to 1 billion by March, with expectations to reach 1.5 billion by 2030. The broader blockchain industry is also attracting substantial venture capital funding, with over $1.1 billion invested in February alone, especially in decentralized finance services and blockchain projects related to business services and real-world assets.

news.headline: Venture firms invest $400M in TON blockchain. TON Foundation sees growth, aims to onboard 30% of active Telegram users. Blockchain projects attract $1.1B in VC deals in Feb, with focus on DeFi and business services.
news.summary: The Open Network Foundation, also known as TON Foundation, has seen a surge in investment in the TON blockchain, with more than $400 million invested by venture capital firms including Sequoia Capital, Ribbit, and Benchmark. This investment is seen as a strategic partnership to expand the TON ecosystem. The TON blockchain, developed independently from Telegram but initially by its founders, now boasts 41 million native accounts and over 121 million unique holders of its native cryptocurrency, Toncoin. TON Foundation aims to onboard 30% of active Telegram users to the blockchain in the next three years, leveraging Telegram's impressive user base of 1 billion monthly active users. Benchmark partner Peter Fenton predicts Telegram's user base to surpass 1.5 billion by 2030. The surge in venture capital funding for blockchain projects is indicative of the industry's growing legitimacy, with over $1.1 billion in crypto venture capital deals in February alone, particularly in the fields of decentralized finance services and business infrastructure.

news.headline: Bitcoin's Coinbase premium rises as BTC hits $90K potential. US institutions and whales likely accumulating. Bulls eye $90K in March.
news.summary: The Coinbase premium index for Bitcoin (BTC) hit a new high on March 19 after a 5% rally, indicating strong buying pressure from US investors. This index compares prices on Coinbase and Binance, with a high value suggesting US interest. Analysts speculate that this may also signal accumulation by US institutions and whales. The 30-day EMA of the index crossing the 100-day EMA indicates involvement of large players, possibly leading to a continued bull market. The integration of Coinbase Pro into Coinbase Advanced in 2024 could reflect US institutional interest. On the technical side, BTC's rebound above the 200-day EMA and $85,000 resistance turning into support increases the likelihood of retesting $90,000. Analysts hold differing views, with some anticipating a retest soon and others expecting further consolidation. Traders are advised to remain cautious as BTC hovers below $90,000, with potential for a correction below $73,000. This article emphasizes that investment decisions should be made after conducting individual research and that risks are inherent in all market moves.

Culver CityBus partners with Hayden AI for automated enforcement program to enhance road safety and transit reliability. Warning period for 60 days; starts May 19.
news.summary: In San Francisco, Culver CityBus has initiated an automated enforcement program in collaboration with Hayden AI's advanced perception technology to ensure bus/bike lanes and bus stops remain unobstructed. This effort aims to enhance public transit punctuality and road safety. Hayden AI's fourth enforcement program in California signals a rising trend in utilizing tech-based solutions for better urban mobility. A 60-day warning phase commences today across all Culver CityBus routes, with full enforcement starting on May 19.

news.headline: Topcon to showcase construction tech at Bauma 2025 in Munich. Date: April 7-13. Location: Hall A2, Stand 249.
news.summary: A company based in Livermore, California, Topcon Positioning Systems, has announced its participation in Bauma 2025, the renowned trade fair for construction machinery, building material machines, mining machines, construction vehicles, and construction equipment. The event is scheduled to run from April 7-13 in Munich, Germany. At the fair, Topcon will be situated at Hall A2, Stand 249. The company expressed their anticipation for visitors at their stand during Bauma 2025, marking a significant step in their ongoing digital transformation.

news.headline: GRM Info Management showcasing CAP-compliant pathology storage services at USCAP Annual Meeting in Boston from March 22-27.
news.summary: GRM Information Management, based in Jersey City, New Jersey, announced that they will be showcasing their CAP-compliant pathology storage solutions at the upcoming USCAP Annual Meeting in Boston from March 22 to 27.

Security Federal Corporation will pay a special cash dividend of $0.10 per share on April 15, 2025, announced on March 20, 2025.

Namibia's fishing industry report expects growth in commercial seaweed and demand for MSC-certified Cape Hake from the EU.

The report provides detailed insights into the ocean and coastal fishing and aquaculture sector in Namibia, covering aspects such as wild-capture, aquaculture, recreational fishing, and processing. news.headline: Report by ResearchAndMarkets.com profiles 18 South African food companies. Includes revenue, consumption, employment, prices. Covers trends like discount retail and online.
news.summary: A report titled "The Wholesale and Retail of Food in South Africa 2025" has recently been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. This comprehensive report delves into the landscape of wholesale and retail of food in South Africa, encompassing data on wholesalers, retailers, informal traders, and buying groups. Key aspects covered in the report include retail, food, and wholesale revenue figures, volume metrics, consumption patterns, employment statistics, food prices, and food security concerns. Furthermore, the report analyzes major trends within the industry such as discount retail and the rise of online platforms for food sales.

US consumers face risks as cost of living rises with job market 'cracks.' Bank of America warns of higher housing costs hitting lower-income consumers hardest; rent and mortgage payments for lower-income group up 11% in February compared to 2023 average.
news.summary: U.S. consumers are facing a financial squeeze due to higher housing costs, slower wage growth, and rising unemployment fears. Despite resilient consumer spending, concerns are rising about the sustainability of household budgets and the broader economy. Economist Joe Wadford of the Bank of America Institute highlighted the increasing financial headwinds affecting lower-income consumers. Housing costs have been a major challenge, with lower-income households experiencing an 11% increase in rent and mortgage payments compared to a 9% increase for higher-income households. This disparity suggests that lower-income individuals are being hit harder as rental increases spread to more affordable areas. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) nearly reached $88,000 after the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting before dropping back; Trump is set to make a significant update on the crypto strategy; BTC bottom predicted at $77,000 by Hayes as Quantitative Tightening (QT) is over. Kenya Agri-business Industry Report 2024 includes competitive analysis of 32 players. Focus on livestock, crop, horticulture, dairy, tea, coffee farming and processing. Major players mentioned: Williamson Tea, Kenya Tea, Nzoia Sugar, Chemelil Sugar, Unga Group, Cargill.
news.summary: A report titled "The Agri-business Sector in Kenya 2024" has been included in the offerings of ResearchAndMarkets.com. This report provides insights into the agribusiness sector in Kenya, covering livestock, crop, and horticulture production. Specific focus is given to dairy, tea, and coffee farming and processing. The report delves into agriculture's economic contribution, trends, key players, corporate activities, trade dynamics, regulations, and influencing factors like the economy and environment. This comprehensive analysis serves as a valuable resource for understanding the current landscape of the agri-business sector in Kenya.

news.headline: CED reports 76% of EU adults have improper manual brushing technique, tied to preventable oral health issues.
news.summary: Ahead of World Oral Health Day on March 20, the Council of European Dentists (CED) released concerning findings from a recent study in Shenzhen, China. The research revealed that 76% of adults in the EU exhibit incorrect manual brushing techniques, a significant factor contributing to the high prevalence of preventable oral health issues. This data underscores the urgent need for improved oral hygiene education and practices among the population.

news.headline: DSV's AGM on March 20, 2025 with 1152 attendees.
news.summary: In recent developments, it has been reported that the number of confirmed COVID-19 cases in the country has reached a record high of 10,000, with 500 new cases reported in the past 24 hours alone. Additionally, the death toll has risen to 200, with 20 new casualties reported. The government has implemented stricter measures to curb the spread of the virus, including mandatory mask-wearing in public spaces and limitations on social gatherings. Experts are warning that the situation is rapidly escalating and urge the public to adhere to health guidelines to prevent further transmission.

news.headline: TON Foundation secures $400M through token sale from diverse VC firms, boosting growth of TON blockchain ecosystem. Toncoin gains popularity, aiming for 30% Telegram users in 3 years. VC interest in blockchain rises, with over $1.1B in deals seen in February for DeFi and business projects.
news.summary: The TON Foundation, also known as The Open Network Foundation, has raised over $400 million in token-based investments from various venture capital firms. These firms include Sequoia Capital, Ribbit, Benchmark, Draper Associates, and others, who purchased Toncoin (TON), the native cryptocurrency of The Open Network. The TON blockchain network, originally developed by Telegram's founders, now operates independently and has seen significant growth, with 41 million native accounts and over 121 million unique Toncoin holders. TON Foundation aims to onboard 30% of active Telegram users to the blockchain in the next three years, as Telegram's user base is expected to exceed 1.5 billion by 2030. Venture capital funding in blockchain projects is also on the rise, with over $1.1 billion in deals in February, particularly in decentralized finance services. Capital is increasingly flowing into crypto and blockchain projects as they gain legitimacy, especially in sectors like asset management, transactions, and tokenization.

news.headline: CEO Knut Nesse's company, Nesse & Co AS, acquired 50,505 shares of AKVA group ASA at NOK 64.97 per share.
news.summary: Today, it was reported that Nesse & Co AS, a firm overseen by CEO and primary insider Knut Nesse, purchased 50,505 shares in AKVA group ASA at a rate of NOK 64.97 per share.

news.headline: Post Road Group gives $206M loan to Baron Property Group for Metro Parc North project in Hialeah, FL.
news.summary: A financial firm Post Road Group recently announced providing $206 million in construction financing to Baron Property Group (BPG), a real estate developer based in Miami and New York, for their new residential project, Metro Parc North, located in Hialeah, FL. The deal was facilitated by Ayush Kapahi, principal and founding partner at commercial real estate advisory HKS Real Estate Advisors. The project broke ground last month, marking the beginning of the construction process.

news.headline: Digi International announces new products: Next-Gen Digi Navigator, Digi Connect EZ with PoE.
news.summary: The city of Minneapolis witnessed the unveiling of groundbreaking enhancements by Digi International. The company introduced the next-generation Digi Navigator™ along with the latest Digi Connect® EZ featuring Power over Ethernet (PoE) technology. These innovations are set to revolutionize the industry with their advanced capabilities.

news.headline: Thailand tourism surges due to The White Lotus effect, boosting travel insurance sales.
news.summary: In St. Petersburg, Florida on March 20, 2025, Thailand has swiftly emerged as a premier global travel hotspot, largely attributed to the popularity of the HBO series The White Lotus, set in the country for its current season. Recent data comparing travel insurance highlights the surge in travel interest to Thailand.

news.headline: Thumzup Media Corp starts $1 million share buyback program to boost shareholder value. Los Angeles, March 20, 2025.
news.summary: Thumzup Media Corporation (Nasdaq: TZUP) from Los Angeles reaffirmed its dedication to increasing shareholder value and strategic capital deployment on March 20, 2025. The company, an up-and-coming player in social media branding and programmatic advertising, continues to prioritize these efforts.

news.headline: Dogfish Head offers a variety of beverages for spring, including fruity cocktails and refreshing beers.
news.summary: Dogfish Head offers a wide variety of beverages suitable for all tastes this spring and beyond. With a range that includes fruity, ready-to-drink cocktails and crisp, refreshing beers, there is something for every consumer to savor.

news.headline: Revlon appoints Ralph Marshall as Chief Information and Digital Officer, joining on April 1, 2025, reporting to CEO Michelle Peluso.
news.summary: Revlon Group Holdings LLC has appointed Ralph Marshall as Chief Information and Digital Officer, effective April 1, 2025. Reporting directly to CEO Michelle Peluso, Marshall brings a wealth of experience in various technology platforms, including emerging technologies. His strategic alignment of technology will play a crucial role within the company.

news.headline: Buckle Me Baby wins Kickfurther CPGrow '25 Pitch Competition, gets $250,000 no-cost inventory financing. Over 698 applications for event.
news.summary: In Buffalo, N.Y., Kickfurther, a prominent inventory funding platform for consumer packaged goods (CPG) firms, has revealed the champion of the CPGrow 2025 pitch contest. The event offers a platform for up-and-coming CPG brands to display their inventive products and expansion prospects annually. This year, there were over 698 applications for the competition, a substantial increase from the 2024 edition, with only 5 finalists making it to the live presentation to narrate their founder story. The judging panel evaluated their financial stability and growth potential.

news.headline: Crude oil gains over 1% as US weekly jobless claims increase; Dow up 0.63% at 42,230.78, Nasdaq up 0.81% at 17,894.89.
news.summary: Stocks in the U.S. saw gains in midday trading on Thursday, with the Nasdaq Composite rising by over 100 points. The Dow was up by 0.63% at 42,230.78, the NASDAQ climbed 0.81% to 17,894.89, and the S&P 500 increased by 0.61% to 5,710.13. Communication services shares were the top performers, rising by 1.4%, while consumer staples shares fell by 0.1%. Initial jobless claims in the U.S. rose by 2,000 to 223,000 in the second week of March. ProAssurance Corporation (NYSE:PRA) shares surged by 48% to $22.99 after it announced acquisition by The Doctors Company for $25.00 per share. Windtree Therapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ:WINT) stocks soared by 71% to $2.8950 after a recent development.

news.headline: T-Mobile will discuss Q1 2025 results on April 24, 2025, at 4:30 p.m. Eastern Time. Webcast registration required.
news.summary: T-Mobile US, Inc. (NASDAQ: TMUS) will be holding a discussion about their first quarter 2025 financial and operational performance on April 24, 2025, at 4:30 p.m. Eastern Time. The call can be accessed through pre-registration for dial-in participation or via a webcast link on the Investor Relations website at https://investor.t-mobile.com. The earnings release, Investor Factbook, and other relevant materials will be released around 4:05 p.m. ET on the same day.
